FinancialDocument.title: FinancialDocument
FinancialDocument.description: Represents a financial document uploaded to the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the financial document.

filename.description: The name of the uploaded file.

uploadDate.description: The date and time the document was uploaded.

documentType.description: The type of the financial document (e.g., bank statement, invoice).

extractedData.description: JSON string containing extracted data from the document using OCR.

category.description: The category of the transaction (e.g., income, expense).

issueDate.description: The date the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: The date the invoice is due.

amount.description: The total amount of the invoice.

status.description: The status of the invoice (e.g., sent, paid, overdue).

customerName.description: The name of the customer the invoice is for.

customerEmail.description: The email of the customer the invoice is for.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Only the user themselves can read/write their profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores financial documents uploaded by the user. Allows the user to manage their financial documents.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: financialDocumentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the financial document.

definition.description: Stores transactions associated with a specific financial document for a user.  Allows the user to manage transactions within a financial document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: financialDocumentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the financial document.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the transaction.

definition.description: Stores invoices created by the user. Allows the user to manage their invoices.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the invoice.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to provide a secure and scalable solution for FinTrack Automation, prioritizing authorization independence, clarity, and ease of debugging. The structure adheres to the principle of storing user-related data under user-specific paths to ensure data privacy and simplified security rules. This design assumes that only authenticated users have access to their own data. The design implements Authorization Independence via Path-Based Ownership:

1.  **FinancialDocuments**: Documents are stored under `/users/{userId}/financialDocuments/{financialDocumentId}`. This allows simple security rules based on `request.auth.uid == userId`. The security rules do not need to `get()` any data from other collections to authorize the request.
2.  **Transactions**: Transactions are stored under `/users/{userId}/financialDocuments/{financialDocumentId}/transactions/{transactionId}`. This allows simple security rules based on `request.auth.uid == userId`. The security rules do not need to `get()` any data from other collections to authorize the request.
3.  **Invoices**: Invoices are stored under `/users/{userId}/invoices/{invoiceId}`. This allows simple security rules based on `request.auth.uid == userId`. The security rules do not need to `get()` any data from other collections to authorize the request.

This design enables secure list operations (QAPs) because all data is scoped to the user's ID. A user can only list documents or transactions within their own user ID space. There is no need to filter documents based on user ID inside the rules. It also allows for atomic batch operations because the rules do not rely on `get()` calls to other documents.
